title: Bio-inspired polymers for use as synthetic mucins
abstract: Provided are glycosylated polymers that derive from carboxymethyl cellulose and may function as synthetic mucins. The carboxymethyl cellulose backbone of the polymers provide structures amenable to glycosylation by amidation of the carboxyl groups with glycosylamine moieties. The polymers can inhibit microbial virulence and may be useful in a variety of applications, such as treating microbial infections, preventing biofilm formation, and improving gastrointestinal health. Thus, provided are compositions (e.g., pharmaceutical, prebiotic, nutraceutical, food product) comprising the polymers, lubricants comprising the polymers, coatings comprising the polymers, methods of inhibiting microbial virulence, methods of inhibiting and/or preventing biofilm formation, methods of treating and/or preventing infection (e.g., bacterial, protozoan, or fungal infection). Also provided are methods of preparing the polymers described herein.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A polymer of formula (I) (I), or a salt thereof, wherein: each occurrence of R is independently hydrogen, -CH 2 C(0)OH, -CH 2 C(0)OR 1 , - CH 2 C(0)NHR 1 , or R 1 ; each occurrence of R 1 is independently a monosaccharide, oligosaccharide, or polysaccharide; and n is equal to or greater than 20; wherein the polymer has, on average, about 0.5 to about 2.0 R groups in each repeat unit that are -CH 2 C(0)OH, -CH 2 C(0)OR 1 , or -CH 2 C(0)NHR 1 . 2. The polymer of claim 1, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a net negative charge at physiological pH. 3. The polymer of claim 1 or 2, or a salt thereof, wherein R is hydrogen, -CH 2 C(0)OH, - CH 2 C(0)OR 1 , or -CH 2 C(0)NHR 1 . 4. The polymer of claim 1 or 2, or a salt thereof, wherein R is hydrogen, -CH 2 C(0)OH, or -CH 2 C(0)NHR 1 . 5. The polymer of any of claims 1-4, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has, on average, about 0.7 to about 1.2 R groups in each repeat unit that are -CH 2 C(0)OH, - CH 2 C(0)OR 1 , or -CH 2 C(0)NHR 1 . 6. The polymer of any of claims 1-5, or a salt thereof, wherein R 1 is a monosaccharide or oligosaccharide. 7. The polymer of any of claims 1-6, or a salt thereof, wherein R is an oligosaccharide. 8. The polymer of any of claims 1-7, or a salt thereof, wherein R is lactose, a human milk oligosaccharide, or a mucin glycan. 9. The polymer of any of claims 1-6, or a salt thereof, wherein R is a monosaccharide 10. The polymer of any of claims 1-6 or 9, or a salt thereof, wherein R is glucose, galactose, mannose, fucose, sialic acid, glucosamine, galactosamine, mannosamine, fucosamine, N-acetylglucosamine, or N-acetylgalactosamine. 11. The polymer of any of claims 1-6, 9 or 10, or a salt thereof, wherein R 1 is glucose, galactose, mannose, or fucose. 12. The polymer of any of claims 1-6 or 9-11, or a salt thereof, wherein R 1 is glucose, galactose, or fucose. 13. The polymer of any of claims 1-6 or 9-11, or a salt thereof, wherein R is hydrogen, - CH C(0)OH, 14. The polymer of any of claims 1-13, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of up to 1,000,000 Da. 15. The polymer of any of claims 1-13, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of up to 300,000 Da. 16. The polymer of any of claims 1-13, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of up to 100,000 Da. 17. The polymer of any of claims 1-13, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of up to 50,000 Da. 18. The polymer of any of claims 1-13, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of up to 20,000 Da. 19. The polymer of any of claims 1-13, or a salt thereof, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of up to 5,000 Da. 20. The polymer of any of claims 1-19, or a salt thereof, wherein the molecular weight of the R 1 groups comprise, by weight, about 1% to about 20% of the molecular weight of the polymer. 21. The polymer of any of claims 1-17, or a salt thereof, wherein the molecular weight of the R 1 groups comprise, by weight, about 5% to about 15% of the molecular weight of the polymer. 22. The polymer of any of claims 1-18, or a salt thereof, wherein the molecular weight of the R 1 groups comprise, by weight, about 10% of the molecular weight of the polymer. 23. A composition comprising the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, and an acceptable carrier. 24. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 25. A prebiotic composition comprising the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 26. A food product comprising the prebiotic composition of claim 25. 27. A lubricant comprising the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the composition of claim 23, or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 24. 28. A method of inhibiting microbial virulence, the method comprising contacting a microbe with the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the composition of claim 23, or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 24. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the microbe is bacteria, archaea, protozoa, fungi, or algae. 30. A method of inhibiting or preventing biofilm formation on a surface, the method comprising coating the surface with the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the composition of claim 23, or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 24. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the biofilm comprises bacteria, archaea, protozoa, fungi, or algae. 32. The method of claim 30 or 31, wherein the biofilm comprises Streptococcus mutatis, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Staphylococcus aureus, or Candida albicans. 33. The method of any of claims 30-32, wherein the biofilm comprises Streptococcus mutans. 34. A method of treating an infection in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the composition of claim 23, or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 24. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the infection is a bacterial, protozoan, or fungal infection. 36. The method of claim 34 or 35, wherein the infection is caused by Streptococcus mutans, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Staphylococcus aureus, or Candida albicans. 37. The method of any of claims 34-36, wherein the infection is caused by Streptococcus mutans. 38. A method of improving gastrointestinal health of a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the prebiotic composition of claim 25, or the food product of claim 26, to the subject. 39. The polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the composition of claim 23, or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 24, for use in the treatment of an infection in a subject in need thereof. 40. The polymer of claim 39, or a salt thereof, wherein the infection is a bacterial, protozoan, or fungal infection. 41. The polymer of claim 39 or 40, or a salt thereof, wherein the infection is caused by Streptococcus mutans, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Staphylococcus aureus, or Candida albicans. 42. The polymer of any of claims 39-41, or a salt thereof, wherein the infection is caused by Streptococcus mutans. 43. A method of preparing the polymer of claim 1, the method comprising coupling carboxymethyl cellulose with one or more monosaccharide, oligosaccharide, or polysaccharide to form the polymer. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the monosaccharide, oligosaccharide, or polysaccharide comprises a glycosylamine. 45. The method of claim 44 further comprising amination of the monosaccharide, oligosaccharide, or polysaccharide to form the glycosylamine. 46. The method of claim 45 further comprising conversion of the acetal of the reducing end of the monosaccharide, oligosaccharide, or polysaccharide to an amino group. 47. The method of claim 45 or 46 further comprising reacting the monosaccharide, oligosaccharide, or polysaccharide with ammonium bicarbonate. 48. The method of any of claims 43-45 further comprising coupling carboxymethyl cellulose with the glycosylamine in an amide bond forming reaction. 49. A kit comprising the polymer of any of claims 1-22, or a salt thereof, the composition of claim 23, the pharmaceutical composition of claim 24, or the prebiotic composition of claim 25; and instructions for administering the polymer, the salt thereof, the prebiotic composition, or the pharmaceutical composition to a subject.
